meaning: Organization of Economic Cooperation and Development,经济合作发展组织
examples: According to the OECD report, some 75% of scholarly journals are now online.
Today, 28 per cent of graduates in the UK are in non-graduate roles, a percentage which is double the average among the OECD countries.
The report, by John Houghton of Victoria University in Australia and Graham Vickery of the OECD, makes heavy reading for publishers who have, so far, made handsome profits.
The IMF and the OECD also see this adjustment as the central problem of the world economy.
But although the UK remains less equal than the OECD average, there has been an improvement.
